title: LarkMemory Benchmark Case Schema v3
description: Standard schema for LarkMemory enterprise memory benchmark test cases. Organized by 4 scenario directions (A/B/C/D), each containing applicable test types (anti_interference, contradiction_update, efficiency, abstention, cross_project).

category.description: Scenario direction this case belongs to (maps to competition tracks A/B/C/D)

test_type.description: Test type — which competition-required or extended capability is being evaluated. Not all test types apply to every direction.

scenario.description: One-sentence description of the business scenario

difficulty.description: Difficulty level. easy=≤7d (same-week active memory), medium=8-90d (cross-sprint/cross-month), hard=>90d (cross-quarter/cross-year). Based on enterprise time anchors and academic memory retention research.

time_span_days.description: Number of days between earliest and latest event in input_events. Used to quantify long-term retention difficulty.

input_events.description: Sequence of events to be ingested into the memory system before querying. Events should form a coherent timeline, not isolated facts.

event_id.description: Unique event identifier within this case (e.g., e1, noise_1)

speaker.description: Name or role of the person who produced this event

content.description: Actual text content of the event

query.description: The retrieval query or task trigger sent to the memory system after ingestion

expected.description: Ground truth for scoring this case. Uses two-layer scoring: answer_correct + evidence_correct.

should_retrieve.description: Whether the system should return a non-empty, relevant result. Set to false for abstention tests.

answer_keywords.description: Keywords that MUST appear in the system's response

should_not_contain.description: Strings that must NOT appear as the CURRENT active answer. For backward compat with simple cases.

current_value.description: For contradiction_update: the correct up-to-date value

inactive_values.description: Historical values that are no longer active. Mentioning them as historical context is OK, but using them as the current answer is WRONG.

forbidden_active_values.description: Values that must NOT be presented as the current/active answer. Stricter than should_not_contain — even partial matches count.

allow_historical_mention.description: If true, mentioning inactive_values in a historical context (e.g., 'previously X, now Y') is considered correct. If false, any mention is penalized.

evidence_event_ids.description: REQUIRED. event_id(s) in input_events that serve as ground-truth evidence. Used for two-layer scoring: answer_correct + evidence_correct.

superseded_event_ids.description: event_id(s) that have been superseded and should NOT be cited as active evidence

suggested_command.description: For command_memory / efficiency: the exact command the system should recommend

baseline_chars.description: For efficiency: character count without memory assistance

actual_chars.description: For efficiency: character count with memory assistance (user input only)

min_saving_rate.description: For efficiency: minimum required character/step saving rate

abstention_keywords.description: For abstention tests: keywords that indicate the system correctly declined to answer (e.g., '未找到', '不确定', '没有相关记忆')

hallucination_triggers.description: For abstention tests: common plausible-but-fabricated answers the system should NOT produce

baseline_steps.description: For efficiency: step count without memory assistance.

actual_steps.description: For efficiency: step count with memory assistance.

metrics.description: List of metric IDs to evaluate for this case
